meta.title: May Thin Lwin | Senior Full-Stack Software Engineer
meta.description: Portfolio of May Thin Lwin, a Senior Full-Stack Software Engineer based in Yangon, Myanmar, specializing in scalable backend systems and enterprise platforms for HR, payroll, and workforce management.

profile.name: May Thin Lwin
profile.title: Senior Full-Stack Software Engineer
profile.tagline: Nearly 9 years building high-performance web applications, microservices, and enterprise systems for corporate groups and international clients.

about.name: about
about.description: Senior full-stack engineer with nearly 9 years of progressively responsible engineering — leading teams and shipping enterprise platforms at scale.
paragraphs: Nearly 9 years of progressively responsible engineering — from owning Laravel + Vue.js + Next.js enterprise platforms and Node.js Temporal workflow workers, to leading a 20-person cross-functional team through architecture, delivery, and people management.

Currently shipping a multi-company HRIS backend serving 36+ companies and 10,000+ payroll runs/month, with measured wins: dashboard load 45s → 500ms, query latency 10s → 50ms. Depth in Laravel, Node.js, and Vue.js; broad working knowledge across the modern PHP / Node / TypeScript stack.

experience.name: experience
experience.description: Leading teams and shipping enterprise platforms since 2017.

projects.name: projects
projects.description: Selected production systems — context, scale, and what I owned.

skills.name: skills
skills.description: Tiered by depth: primary (daily production-grade), production-confident, and project-level exposure.

contact.name: contact
contact.description: Have a project or opportunity? Send a message — I typically respond within 24 hours.

bootLines.output: Laravel · Node.js · Vue.js (depth) · Next.js · TypeScript · PostgreSQL
HRIS · 36+ companies · 10k payroll runs/mo · 90× faster dashboard and queue processing

experience.description: Lead engineer on legacy-to-modern HRIS migration — platform architecture, API design, and workflow orchestration.
highlights: Cut dashboard load time from 45s → 500ms (≈90× faster) on complex relational metrics via query optimization and caching strategy.
Cut hot-path query latency from 10s → 50ms (≈200× faster) — paths consumed directly by the mobile client.
Engineered Node.js Temporal workflow workers for durable async processing: bulk imports/exports, leave generation, payroll runs, notifications.
Built dynamic, role-driven approval workflows spanning Laravel and Node.js service boundaries.
Designed secure AWS S3 asset pipelines for file, image, and document upload/download flows.
Implemented policy-driven leave engine: prorata accrual, rollover, auto-approve, balance checks.

experience.description: Six years leading cross-functional teams shipping event booking, e-learning, and complaint management systems for corporate clients.
highlights: Led a 20-person cross-functional team for 6 years; directly managed 13 engineers.
Owned architecture and tech-stack decisions across enterprise engagements (Phalcon, Zend, Spring Boot, Oracle, MSSQL).
Built event booking & management systems with visitor registration and data export workflows.
Shipped an interactive online learning platform with gamification and Line app integration.
Engineered a broadband complaint management system on Zend + Oracle.

projects.title: Enterprise HRIS Platform (Refactoring & Modernization)
projects.context: Multi-company HRIS for HR teams and employee self-service — attendance, leave, payroll, overtime. Built on Laravel REST APIs, Vue 3 frontend, and Node.js Temporal workflow workers, per Myanmar payroll compliance (NRC, SSB, tax claims).
projects.scale: 36+ companies · 10,000+ payroll runs/mo · dashboard 45s → 500ms · queries 10s → 50ms · mobile clients consuming the API
features: Architected Laravel REST APIs using Domain-Driven Design with type-safe Spatie Data DTOs and generated TypeScript contracts.
Integrated Temporal workflow workers for durable async processing: bulk imports/exports, leave generation, payroll runs, notifications.
Multi-auth: OTP, biometric, Azure AD, Microsoft, device login for mobile and kiosk.
Role/module-driven admin platform for HR configuration.

projects.title: HR Admin Platform (Multi-Company Workforce Management)
projects.context: Enterprise HR admin platform — employees, leave, attendance, payroll, and internal comms in one web workspace.
projects.scale: Multi-company · policy-driven leave engine · payroll pipeline
features: Laravel Queue Horizon for background job processing.
Policy-driven leave engine: prorata accrual, rollover, auto-approve, balance checks.
Payroll pipeline: generation, payslips, income tax, partial payments.
Bulk import/export for employees, leave, attendance, payroll data.

projects.title: Software House Marketing & Client Portal (Freelance)
projects.context: Mobile-first responsive web app for a software house — marketing site plus client-engagement portal. Showcases previous work, partners, team, vision, and mission; lets prospective customers connect directly with the team.
projects.scale: Public marketing site + client contact flow · mobile-first · React + Next.js stack
features: Mobile-first responsive layouts across all pages.
Project showcase: previous works, partners, and team members.
Direct client-to-team contact flow for new project inquiries.

projects.title: Point of Sales (POS) System
projects.context: POS system with inventory, stock control, sales tracking, and admin dashboard.

projects.title: Online Learning Platform
projects.context: Interactive grade-based book learning — reporting, commenting, rating, and gamification.

projects.title: E-Commerce Backend
projects.context: E-commerce backend — product catalog, order processing, card/cash payments, home delivery, admin-customer data exchange.

terminal.welcomeMessage: Welcome to May Thin Lwin's portfolio terminal. Type "help" to see available commands.
terminal.about: Senior Full-Stack Software Engineer with nearly 9 years building scalable backends and leading teams. Depth in Laravel, Node.js, and Vue.js. Based in Yangon, Myanmar.
terminal.skills:
Primary: Laravel, Node.js, Vue.js
Production: TypeScript, React, Next.js, PostgreSQL, Redis, AWS S3, Docker
Familiar: Spring Boot, NestJS, Nuxt.js, Temporal, Phalcon, Zend

what_changed: experience[1].role: 'Full Stack Developer · Team Lead' → 'Tech Lead / Engineering Manager' (matches 20-person team scope).
experience[1].description: 1 vague sentence → scope + team + tech-stack context.
experience[0].highlights: 4 qualitative bullets → 6 quantified bullets (90×, 200×, payroll scale, etc.).
skills: flat 4-category list → 3-tier depth model (primary / production-confident / familiar) — defends the 7+/10 claim.
skills: React + Next.js moved from `familiar` to `production-confident` (backed by the new freelance project entry).
projects: added `role` and `scale` fields so readers see context, not just features.
projects: added 'Software House Marketing & Client Portal (Freelance)' entry (React + Next.js, mobile-first) — placed at slot 3 to surface breadth right after the two Onenex enterprise projects.
heroTerminal: replaced generic skills line with stack + impact line.
Removed phone number from public profile (privacy) — replace with contact form if needed.
still_missing: Education / certifications — add if you have any (AWS cert, degree, etc.).
GitHub URL — currently '#'. Fill in or remove.
METATEAM project-specific scale numbers — you said you don't remember; the rewritten section is honest about that and leads with leadership scope instead.

Cron-job metric — your phrasing '10 jobs per milliseconds' is mathematically 10k jobs/sec, which is not real. Clarify before it goes on a CV: probably 'cron throughput improved ~60×' or '60 jobs/min → 600 jobs/min' or similar.
